discussion.tldr: The Discussion area was dominated by a debate over the new Trading Hub "terminal" and what a "verifiable track record" actually means — Barncore/mgm828 pressed Zero on third-party verification vs a self-hosted DB, while rkrd_crypto and Zero defended documenting every trade at $10/mo (with some subscription-cancellation drama and repeated calls to keep it civil). Trade-wise: DOGE spot holders nursed ~50% drawdowns and debated DCA vs cutting (eyeing 0.06), broad interest in OIL/USOIL monthly-gap longs (66–70) + CONOCO, silver (XAG) ran near full TP, and Zero shared a manual NFLX short. ORCL was flagged as a fresh HTF IVB. Members floated MU, PLTR and LYFT; Wendy's pumped ~50%.

topics.topic: What 'verifiable track record' means for the new terminal
topics.summary: Long debate: Barncore/mgm828 argued a self-hosted DB isn't third-party-verifiable; rkrd_crypto and Zero countered that every entry/exit is documented and screenshot-checkable for $10/mo. Some cancellation drama; members asked to keep it respectful.
Spot holders down ~50% (buy-ins ~0.102) debated infinite-DCA vs capitulating; some cut at -50%, others wait for 0.06. Zero: spot crypto setups come when BTC hits its levels.
Multiple members building low-leverage/spot oil longs into the monthly gap (66–70 area, USO confluence); Zero says the setups he's watching are US OIL + CONOCO in the terminal.
XAG ran close to full TP; Zero flagged 55/50 as a good HTF discount area for spot.
Zero flagged Oracle as a potential HTF IVB; the Hub published an ORCL LONG approaching the VAH/POC of a prior fair value after a -38% correction.

topics.topic: Zero shares a manual NFLX short
topics.summary: Zero shared a manual NFLX short setup (2D level respected, 1:3) outside the terminal; SL below demand, TPs evaluated step by step.
WEN spiked ~50% in a day; members who had it as a priority/momentum play discussed the move and pullback.

ideas.note: Posted a MU chart — floated Micron (semiconductors) as an idea.

ideas.note: Asked Zero if PLTR 'might be getting interesting'.

ideas.note: Suggested LYFT as a similar play to Zero's Uber chart (HTF BB).

ideas.note: Flagged Wendy's ~50% one-day pump; members had it as a momentum play.

payload.entry: Approaching the VAH and POC of the prior fair value after a -38% correction (HTF IVB); 3W/6W demand sits just below the POC as coverage.
payload.invalidation: Performing badly / melting the demands and closing below the VAL.
payload.sharia_note: Large-cap software; Oracle carries high interest-bearing debt — verify the debt ratio (likely fails the 33% screen) on Zoya/Musaffa.
payload.note: LONG but leverage 1x = a spot-compatible cash stock buy. Strong HTF method (IVB + supply/demand) with clear invalidation, but no numeric entry/stop and it's a pre-confirmation knife-catch (-38% off highs).
payload.caption: ORCL LONG — HTF IVB at VAH/POC after a -38% correction
payload.text: Oracle exited a 70-day fair value and after another 70 days (and a -38% correction) is approaching the VAH and the POC of the previous fair value. Based on the setup this is a valid HTF IVB. Exit: trailing and taking most at the $200 psychological level + WFVG rebalancement + 0.5 fib. Strategies: IVB, Supply/Demand.
